suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a commit scoping convention: prefer single-feature-per-commit to make isolated review and rollback tractable. The source-preset-UX commit bundled unrelated infrastructure changes (webhook secret autofill, Google access token forwarding, recovery copy, tunnel banner), making the diff harder to review.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Commit 4141468 bundles changes across app.js (297), index.html (84), style.css (49), user-content-store.js (25). Unrelated changes include autofillDiscoveryWebhookSecretFromBootstrap, getDiscoveryRequestGoogleAccessToken, recovery copy refactoring, tunnel banner.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether 'existing server-side tests cover the assertion' satisfies the 'add or update failing tests first (red)' step, or if browser-side tests are always required for UI/UX assertions. The browser-discovery-preset-ux-and-persistence handoff showed tests.added: [] despite the skill requiring browser-side tests for the six VAL-UI-SEL assertions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Handoff for browser-discovery-preset-ux-and-persistence shows tests.added: [], tests.updated: []. discovery-frontend-worker SKILL.md step 3 says 'Add or update failing tests first (red) for the changed behavior' without clarifying scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Commit 4141468 for browser-discovery-preset-ux-and-persistence bundles unrelated feature changes (webhook secret autofill, Google access token forwarding, recovery copy refactoring, tunnel banner) in a single commit. Other commits in this repo follow a single-feature-per-commit pattern. AGENTS.md does not document commit scoping convention.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show 4141468 --stat shows changes across app.js, index.html, style.css, user-content-store.js. Unrelated changes: autofillDiscoveryWebhookSecretFromBootstrap, getDiscoveryRequestGoogleAccessToken, recovery copy, tunnel banner.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The discovery-source-preset-contract-and-fallback worker added googleAccessToken field to JSON schema and contracts without updating AGENTS.md or mission.md to document this new optional webhook payload field.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: schemas/discovery-webhook-request.v1.schema.json adds googleAccessToken property; contracts.ts DiscoveryWebhookRequestV1 adds googleAccessToken field. Neither AGENTS.md nor mission.md mentions this field.

sharedStateObservations.observation: normalizeSourcePreset has a dual-location pattern: app.js defines its own local copy and user-content-store.js exports one. The architectural split isn't documented — when should browser JS use the local copy vs the UC export? SOURCE_PRESET_VALUES enum is duplicated in three locations (app.js local, user-content-store.js, syncSourcePresetUi VALID_PRESETS) creating maintenance risk.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app.js:16026 defines local normalizeSourcePreset with inline SOURCE_PRESET_VALUES. user-content-store.js:269 defines module-level normalizeSourcePreset with module-level SOURCE_PRESET_VALUES. syncSourcePresetUi defines VALID_PRESETS.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The discovery-integration-worker SKILL.md procedure was followed correctly for discovery-source-preset-contract-and-fallback (red-first tests, minimal implementation, pre-handoff validators). No deviations.
